title: crepidium langkawiense
intro: Crepidium langkawiense, also known as the Langkawi crepidium, is a species of orchid native to the Langkawi Islands in Malaysia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating pain. In herbal preparations, Crepidium langkawiense is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, and is also used in traditional remedies for various ailments.
